1.texto_original: Justo [eres] tú, oh Jehová, cuando yo contigo disputo; sin embargo hablaré contigo de [tus] juicios. ¿Por qué es prosperado el camino de los impíos, y tienen bien todos los que se portan deslealmente?
1.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremías 12:1

"Justo [eres] tú, oh Jehová, cuando yo contigo disputo; sin embargo hablaré contigo de [tus] juicios. ¿Por qué es prosperado el camino de los impíos, y tienen bien todos los que se portan deslealmente?"

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
1.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Jeremías 12:1

Philological Analysis

"Justo [eres] tú, oh Jehová, cuando yo contigo disputo; sin embargo hablaré contigo de [tus] juicios. ¿Por qué es prosperado el camino de los impíos, y tienen bien todos los que se portan deslealmente?"

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.
1.ninos:
🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Justo [eres] tú, oh Jehová, cuando yo contigo disputo; sin embargo hablaré contigo de [tus] juicios. ¿Por qué es prosperado el camino de los impíos, y tienen bien todos los que se portan deslealmente?"

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

🌟 Amazing Story! 🌈

Hey kids! 🚀 Today we learn something super cool from the Bible! ✨

"Así dice Jehová contra todos mis malos vecinos, que tocan la heredad que hice poseer a mi pueblo Israel: He aquí que yo los arrancaré de su tierra, y arrancaré de en medio de ellos la casa de Judá."

This verse teaches us something really important about God 💝. He loves us SO much! 🌟

Think about it: just like an artist 🎨 carefully paints every detail, God carefully created everything — including YOU! 😊

Fun Activity: Draw your favorite thing God created! 🖍️🙏❤️

15.texto_original: Y será que, después que los hubiere arrancado, tornaré y tendré misericordia de ellos, y los haré volver cada uno a su heredad, y cada cual a su tierra.
15.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremías 12:15

"Y será que, después que los hubiere arrancado, tornaré y tendré misericordia de ellos, y los haré volver cada uno a su heredad, y cada cual a su tierra."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
15.academico:
Exegetical Analysis of Jeremías 12:15

Philological Analysis

"Y será que, después que los hubiere arrancado, tornaré y tendré misericordia de ellos, y los haré volver cada uno a su heredad, y cada cual a su tierra."

The original language presents significant lexical and syntactic elements. Key terms reveal theological distinctions often lost in translation.

Historical-Critical Context

Source criticism and form criticism illuminate the compositional history. The Sitz im Leben reflects socio-religious dynamics of antiquity.

Theological Implications

This verse contributes to covenant theology, divine sovereignty, human responsibility, and eschatological hope.

Archaeological Evidence

Recent discoveries from the ancient Near East provide material context for interpreting this passage.

16.texto_original: Y será que, si cuidadosamente aprendieren los caminos de mi pueblo, para jurar en mi nombre, diciendo: Vive Jehová, así como enseñaron a mi pueblo a jurar por Baal; ellos serán prosperados en medio de mi pueblo.
16.normal:
Devotional Study of Jeremías 12:16

"Y será que, si cuidadosamente aprendieren los caminos de mi pueblo, para jurar en mi nombre, diciendo: Vive Jehová, así como enseñaron a mi pueblo a jurar por Baal; ellos serán prosperados en medio de mi pueblo."

This verse carries deep theological significance. The original Hebrew/Greek text reveals layers of meaning that illuminate Gods character and purposes. The context within the broader narrative provides essential framework for understanding.

The historical backdrop adds richness to interpretation. Archaeological discoveries continue to confirm and illuminate the cultural setting in which these words were written.

For the modern reader, this verse offers both comfort and challenge. It invites us to trust in Gods sovereign plan while actively living out its principles in daily life.
